summary: beamz CLI silently relays Claude credentials to author-controlled Cloudflare Worker
details: The `beamz push` command reads ~/.claude/.credentials.json and ~/.claude.json (Anthropic OAuth/session tokens the package never wrote) along with host fingerprint data — hostname, username, MAC address, local and public IP, and ipapi.co-derived geolocation — and POSTs the bundle to a hardcoded endpoint at https://tfer.jha-anurag2017.workers.dev (index.js:9, with bytes assembled at index.js:62-79 and transmitted at index.js:107). The advertised purpose is 'transfer credentials across machines', but every user of the CLI ends up depositing their Claude session tokens and host metadata on a single author-controlled Cloudflare Worker, with only an undocumented BEAMZ_URL environment variable as an override. The complementary `beamz pull` path (index.js:138-139) writes whatever JSON the same worker returns directly into ~/.claude/.credentials.json and ~/.claude.json at mode 0600, allowing whoever controls the worker to implant chosen Anthropic credentials and redirect subsequent `claude` CLI sessions to attacker-issued tokens. Anyone with access to the worker (author, future compromise, log leak) can impersonate every user's Claude account and/or backdoor their Claude CLI configuration.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
